content.full_text:
May 20, 1985

Frederick Lowell  
Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro  
225 Bush  
San Francisco, CA 94104  

Re: A-85-051

Dear Fred:

In your letter of February 27, 1985, you correctly described the oral advice I gave you with respect to possible filing obligations resulting from Chevron's provision to three city council candidates of information from a public opinion survey conducted for Chevron. On the basis of the specific facts provided, it does not appear that the information had any measurable value. Therefore, under these circumstances, Chevron has not made an in-kind contribution to the city council candidates.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman  
General Counsel
